'''''UFC 256: Figueiredo vs. Moreno''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on December 12, 2020 at the [[UFC Apex]] facility in [[Enterprise, Nevada]], part of the [[Las Vegas–Paradise, NV MSA|Las Vegas Metropolitan Area]], United States.<ref name="Nunes vs. Anderson">{{Cite web|url=https://www.si.com/mma/2020/08/21/amanda-nunes-defend-title-megan-anderson-ufc-256|title=Report: Amanda Nunes to defend featherweight title in December bout vs. Megan Anderson|publisher=si.com|author=Nick Selbe|date=2020-08-21|access-date=2020-08-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/2020-ufc-event-schedule-deiveson-figueiredo-vs-alex-perez-curtis-blaydes-vs-derrick-lewis-among-events/|title=2020 UFC event schedule: Deiveson Figueiredo vs. Alex Perez, Curtis Blaydes vs. Derrick Lewis among events|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Brent Brookhouse|date=2020-11-15|access-date=2020-11-19}}</ref>

==Background==
The promotion was initially targeting a [[UFC Welterweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Kamaru Usman]] (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: American Top Team vs. Blackzilians]] welterweight winner) and [[Gilbert Burns (fighter)|Gilbert Burns]] to serve as the event headliner.<ref name="KUGB">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/9/1/21417296/kamaru-usman-vs-gilbert-burns-targeted-for-ufc-256-in-december|title=Kamaru Usman vs. Gilbert Burns targeted for UFC 256 in December|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2020-09-01|access-date=2020-09-02}}</ref> The pairing was previously scheduled to meet in July at [[UFC 251]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/06/ufc-251-kamaru-usman-vs-gilbert-burns-booked-to-headline|title=Kamaru Usman vs. Gilbert Burns headlines UFC 251 on 'Fight Island' in Abu Dhabi|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2020-06-09|access-date=2020-09-02}}</ref> However, Burns was pulled from the contest after he and his coach, Greg Jones, [[COVID-19 testing|tested positive for COVID-19]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/07/ufc-251-gilbert-burns-out-kamaru-usman-title-fight-coronavirus|title=Gilbert Burns tests positive for COVID-19, out of UFC 251 headliner vs. Kamaru Usman|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2020-07-03|access-date=2020-09-02}}</ref> In turn, Usman faced, and defeated [[Jorge Masvidal]] at that event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/29449667/kamaru-usman-beats-jorge-masvidal-via-unanimous-decision-ufc-251-retain-welterweight-belt|title=Kamaru Usman beats Jorge Masvidal via unanimous decision at UFC 251 to retain welterweight belt|work=espn.com|author=Marc Raimondi|date=2020-07-12|access-date=2020-09-02}}</ref> On October 5, it was reported Usman pulled out from this bout, citing more time needed to recover from undisclosed injuries and the bout was postponed.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/30048745/sources-kamaru-usman-gilbert-burns-fight-happen-dec-12|title=Sources: Usman-Burns fight on Dec. 12 is off|work=espn.com|author=Ariel Helwani|date=2020-10-05|access-date=2020-10-06}}</ref>

While not officially announced by the organization, the promotion was also targeting a [[UFC Women's Featherweight Championship]] bout between the current champion [[Amanda Nunes]] (also the current [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship|UFC Women's Bantamweight Champion]]) and former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Featherweight Championship|Invicta FC Featherweight Champion]] [[Megan Anderson (fighter)|Megan Anderson]] to serve as the event headliner.<ref name="Nunes vs. Anderson" /> In turn, it was announced on November 9 that Nunes pulled out due to an undisclosed injury and the bout was postponed to 2021.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/megan-anderson-vs-amanda-nunes-off-ufc-256-title-fight-pushed-2021-injury|title=Megan Anderson says Amanda Nunes out of UFC 256, title fight pushed to 2021|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2020-11-09|access-date=2020-11-09}}</ref>

A [[UFC Bantamweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Petr Yan]] and [[Aljamain Sterling]] was originally scheduled to serve as the co-main event.<ref name="BWtitle">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/10/ufc-256-petr-yan-vs-aljamain-sterling-bantamweight-title-fight|title=Petr Yan vs. Aljamain Sterling bantamweight title fight set for UFC 256|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2020-10-14|access-date=2020-10-14}}</ref> Due to the aforementioned cancelations, they were then expected to headline the card. On November 22, Russian media revealed that the fight was also canceled for personal reasons related to Yan and is expected to be booked at a later date.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://tass.ru/sport/10069613|title=Yan vs. Sterling UFC title fight canceled|publisher=tass.ru|author=Staff|date=2020-11-22|access-date=2020-11-22}} {{in lang|ru}}</ref> Those "personal reasons" were later revealed to be related to [[travel visa|visa]] and travel issues.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/11/24/21612438/petr-yan-reveals-why-he-withdrew-from-scheduled-title-defense-against-aljamain-sterling-at-ufc-256|title=Morning Report: Petr Yan reveals why he withdrew from scheduled title defense against Aljamain Sterling at UFC 256|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Jed Meshew|date=2020-11-24|access-date=2020-11-24}}</ref>

Following his first title defense at [[UFC 255]] on November 21, [[UFC Flyweight Championship|UFC Flyweight Champion]] [[Deiveson Figueiredo]] headlined this event against [[Brandon Moreno]], who also fought at that same date. This booking represented the shortest turnaround between title fights by a champion in UFC history at just 21 days.<ref name="FWtitle">{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/30363749/deiveson-figueiredo-brandon-moreno-agree-fight-ufc-256|title=Sources: Deiveson Figueiredo, Brandon Moreno agree to fight at UFC 256|work=espn.com|author=Ariel Helwani|date=2020-11-22|access-date=2020-11-22}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ufc.com/news/main-co-main-events-confirmed-ufc-256-Deiveson-Figueiredo-vs-Brandon-Moreno-Tony-Ferguson-vs-Charles-Oliveira|title=Main & co-main events confirmed for UFC 256|publisher=ufc.com|author=Staff and Vinicius Giglio|date=2020-11-24|access-date=2020-11-24}}</ref> Former [[Rizin Fighting Federation#Bantamweight Championship|Rizin FF Bantamweight Champion]] [[Manel Kape]], who was expected to make his promotional debut a week later at [[UFC Fight Night: Thompson vs. Neal]], served as a potential replacement.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://empiresportsmedia.com/mma/manel-kape-to-serve-as-ufc-256-backup/|title=Manel Kape to serve as UFC 256 backup|publisher=empiresportsmedia.com|author=Daniel Cunningham|date=2020-12-05|access-date=2020-12-05}}</ref>

Former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Strawweight Championship|Invicta FC]] and [[UFC Women's Strawweight Championship|UFC Women's Strawweight Champion]] [[Carla Esparza]] (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: A Champion Will Be Crowned]] women's strawweight winner) was briefly scheduled to face [[Amanda Ribas]] at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://globoesporte.globo.com/combate/noticia/ultimate-planeja-duelo-entre-amanda-ribas-e-carla-esparza-para-o-ufc-256-em-dezembro.ghtml|title=UFC plans duel between Amanda Ribas and Carla Esparza for UFC 256 in December|publisher=globoesporte.globo.com|author=Staff|date=2020-09-09|access-date=2020-09-09}} {{in lang|pt}}</ref> However on October 9, it was announced that Esparza was pulled due to undisclosed reasons.<ref name="Esparza out">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/10/9/21509569/carla-esparza-out-ufc-256-fight-amanda-ribas|title=Carla Esparza no longer fighting Amanda Ribas at UFC 256|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz, Damon Martin and Mike Heck|date=2020-10-09|access-date=2020-10-09}}</ref> Ribas was rescheduled against former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Atomweight Championship|Invicta FC Atomweight Champion]] [[Michelle Waterson]] at [[UFC 257]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/nfl/story/_/id/30246866/michelle-waterson-amanda-ribas-closing-deal-fight-ufc-257-sources-say|title=Michelle Waterson, Amanda Ribas closing in on deal to fight at UFC 257, sources say|work=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto|date=2020-11-02|access-date=2020-11-09}}</ref>

A middleweight rematch between [[Omari Akhmedov]] and [[Marvin Vettori]] was briefly linked to the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Marvin-Vettori-Rematches-Omari-Akhmedov-at-UFC-256-on-Dec-12-175309|title=Marvin Vettori rematches Omari Akhmedov at UFC 256 on Dec. 12|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Jay Pettry|date=2020-09-25|access-date=2020-10-13}}</ref> The pairing met previously at [[UFC 219]] in December 2017, when they fought to a majority draw.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2017/12/omari-akhmedov-marvin-vettori-majority-draw-slugfest-ufc-219-results|title=UFC 219 results: Marvin Vettori, Omari Akhmedov slug their way to majority draw|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Ben Fowlkes|date=2017-12-30|access-date=2020-10-13}}</ref> On October 13, it was announced that Akhmedov pulled out due to undisclosed reasons and was replaced by former [[List of Strikeforce champions#Middleweight Championship|Strikeforce Middleweight Champion]] [[Ronaldo Souza]].<ref name="OAout">{{Cite web|url=https://fight.theshieldofwrestling.com/marvin-vettori-affrontera-jacare-souza-ad-ufc-256/|title=Marvin Vettori will face Jacaré Souza at UFC 256|publisher=fight.theshieldofwrestling.com|author=Andrea Ruggini|date=2020-10-13|access-date=2020-10-13|archive-date=2021-07-31|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210731193516/https://fight.theshieldofwrestling.com/marvin-vettori-affrontera-jacare-souza-ad-ufc-256/|url-status=dead}} {{in lang|it}}</ref> In turn, Vettori was removed from the contest to serve as a replacement for the main event of [[UFC on ESPN: Hermansson vs. Vettori]] a week earlier.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/ufc-fight-night-jack-hermansson-vs-marvin-vettori-middleweight-main-event-dec-5|title=Jack Hermansson vs. Marvin Vettori set as new UFC on ESPN 19 headliner|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn and John Morgan|date=2020-11-28|access-date=2020-11-28}}</ref> [[Kevin Holland]], whom Vettori replaced due to a [[COVID-19 testing|COVID-19 positive test]], was rescheduled to face Souza at this event.<ref name="Holland">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/kevin-holland-vs-ronaldo-jacare-souza-ufc-256-fight-booking-mma|title=Kevin Holland vs. 'Jacare' Souza added to UFC 256|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Dave Doyle|date=2020-11-29|access-date=2020-11-29}}</ref>

A featherweight bout between [[Billy Quarantillo]] and [[Gavin Tucker]] took place at this event.<ref name="retake">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/10/ufc-256-news-billy-quarantillo-vs-gavin-tucker-mma-fight-booking|title=Billy Quarantillo and Gavin Tucker get their wishes, booked to fight at UFC 256|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2020-10-28|access-date=2020-10-28}}</ref> The pairing was previously scheduled to meet at an [[Canceled UFC event on April 25, 2020|event on April 25]] that was cancelled due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/03/billy-quarantillo-vs-gavin-tucker-added-to-ufc-on-espn-31|title=Billy Quarantillo vs. Gavin Tucker added to UFC on ESPN+ 31|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2020-03-05|access-date=2020-10-28}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/4/9/21215721/ufc-249-upcoming-events-cancelled-due-to-coronavirus-dana-white-promises-fight-island-will-proceed|title=UFC 249, upcoming events postponed due to coronavirus pandemic, Dana White promises 'Fight Island' will proceed|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2020-04-09|access-date=2020-04-09}}</ref>

[[Renato Moicano]] and [[Rafael Fiziev]] were expected to meet in a lightweight bout at [[UFC on ESPN: Smith vs. Clark]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://globoesporte.globo.com/combate/noticia/ultimate-acerta-renato-moicano-x-rafael-fiziev-para-o-card-do-dia-28-de-novembro.ghtml|title=Ultimate sets Renato Moicano x Rafael Fiziev for November 28th card|publisher=globoesporte.globo.com|author=Raphael Marinho|date=2020-09-27|access-date=2020-11-21}} {{in lang|pt}}</ref> In turn, Moicano pulled out after testing positive for COVID-19 and the bout was rescheduled for this event.<ref name="rescheduled">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/11/21/21589045/renato-moicano-tests-positive-for-covid-19-fight-against-rafael-fiziev-rescheduled-for-ufc-256|title=Renato Moicano tests positive for COVID-19, fight against Rafael Fiziev rescheduled for UFC 256|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin and Guilherme Cruz|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>

[[Andrea Lee (fighter)|Andrea Lee]] was expected to face [[Gillian Robertson]] in a women's flyweight bout at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/ufc-256-andrea-lee-vs-gillian-robertson-fight-booking|title=Andrea Lee vs. Gillian Robertson in the works for UFC 256|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura and John Morgan|date=2020-11-20|access-date=2020-12-06}}</ref> However, Lee pulled out in early December due to a broken nose and Robertson was rescheduled to face [[Taila Santos]] at [[UFC Fight Night: Thompson vs. Neal]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2020/12/05/gillian-robertson-now-fights-taila-santos-ufc-vegas-17/|title=Gillian Robertson now fights Taila Santos at UFC Vegas 17|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Jay Anderson|date=2020-12-05|access-date=2020-12-06}}</ref>

A week before the event, several fights were impacted by the [[COVID-19 pandemic]]:
*A women's strawweight rematch between former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Strawweight Championship|Invicta FC Strawweight Champion]] [[Angela Hill (fighter)|Angela Hill]] and [[Tecia Torres]] was expected to take place at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/10/19/21523662/tecia-torres-vs-angela-hill-rematch-booked-for-ufc-256|title=Tecia Torres vs. Angela Hill rematch booked for UFC 256|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin and Mike Heck|date=2020-10-19|access-date=2020-12-07}}</ref> The pairing met previously at [[UFC 188]] in June 2015, when Torres won by unanimous decision.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2015/06/ufc-188-results-tecia-torres-outworks-tuf-20-teammate-angela-hill-for-decision-win|title=UFC 188 results: Tecia Torres outworks 'TUF 20' teammate Angela Hill for decision win|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Ben Fowlkes|date=2015-06-13|access-date=2020-12-07}}</ref> However, Hill tested positive on December 6 and was removed from the bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/12/ufc-256-angela-hill-positive-covid-19-test-tecia-torres-rematch|title=Angela Hill announces positive COVID-19 test, out of Tecia Torres rematch at UFC 256|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2020-12-06|access-date=2020-12-07}}</ref> Torres faced promotional newcomer [[Sam Hughes (fighter)|Sam Hughes]].<ref name=Torreshughes>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/12/8/22163636/tecia-torres-will-now-face-lfa-standout-sam-hughes-at-ufc-256|title=Tecia Torres will now face LFA standout Sam Hughes at UFC 256|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee and Damon Martin|date=2020-12-08|access-date=2020-12-08}}</ref>

*[[Karl Roberson]] and [[Dalcha Lungiambula]] were expected to meet in a middleweight bout at this card, but were eventually rescheduled for the following week's event due to Roberson's positive test.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.fightful.com/mma/report-karl-roberson-faces-dalcha-lungiambula-ufc-256|title=Report: Karl Roberson faces Dalcha Lungiambula at UFC 256|publisher=fightful.com|author=David Tees|date=2020-11-07|access-date=2020-12-07}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/12/ufc-fight-night-183-three-late-additions-booked|title=UFC Fight Night 183 reaches 16 bouts after Gillian Robertson vs. Taila Santos, two others added|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2020-12-07|access-date=2020-12-07}}</ref>

*[[Dwight Grant]] also tested positive for COVID-19 during fight week and had to pull out of his welterweight bout against [[Li Jingliang]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/12/ufc-256-news-dwight-grant-out-vs-li-jingliang-covid-19-test|title=Dwight Grant announces positive COVID-19 test, out of UFC 256 fight vs. Li Jingliang|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2020-12-07|access-date=2020-12-08}}</ref> A replacement wasn't able to be found and therefore Li was removed from the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmanews.com/tecia-torres-gets-new-opponent-for-ufc-256-li-jingliang-doesnt/|title=Tecia Torres gets new opponent for UFC 256, Li Jingliang doesn't|publisher=mmanews.com|author=Cole Shelton|date=2020-12-09|access-date=2020-12-09}}</ref>

*Two days before the event, [[Jared Vanderaa]] also pulled out of the event due to a positive test. He was scheduled to face [[Sergey Spivak]] in a heavyweight bout.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/11/sergey-spivak-vs-jared-vanderaa-in-the-works-for-ufc-256|title=Sergey Spivak vs. Jared Vanderaa in the works for UFC 256|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura and Farah Hannoun|date=2020-11-06|access-date=2020-12-10}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2020/12/10/22167900/sergey-spivak-vs-jared-vanderaa-removed-from-ufc-256-due-to-positive-covid-19-test|title=Sergey Spivak vs. Jared Vanderaa removed from UFC 256 due to positive COVID-19 test|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2020-12-10|access-date=2020-12-10}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2020/12/13/22171568/ufc-256-official-bonus-winners-results-mma-espn-ppv-figueiredo-moreno-ferguson-vegas-holland-fiziev|title=UFC 256 bonuses: Kevin Holland leads $50K winners with KO off his back|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Stephie Haynes|date=2020-12-12|access-date=2020-12-12}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Deiveson Figueiredo vs. Brandon Moreno'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Kevin Holland''' and '''Rafael Fiziev'''

==Aftermath==
This event won the UFC Honors ''2020 Event of the Year'' award as voted by the fans.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=215780776815276|title=#UFC256 earns the #UFCHonors Fan Choice 'Event of the Year'|author=UFC|publisher=UFC|date=January 12, 2021}}</ref>
